sn38.1:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Sāriputta was staying in Magadha near Nāla Village.
sn38.1:1.2: Then Jambukhādaka the wanderer went to Ven. Sāriputta and, on arrival, exchanged courteous greetings with him.
sn38.1:1.3: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there he said to Ven. Sāriputta:
sn38.1:2.1: “‘Unbinding, unbinding,’ it is said, my friend Sāriputta.
sn38.1:2.3: “The ending of passion, the ending of aversion, the ending of delusion:
sn38.1:2.5: “But is there a path, is there a practice for the realization1 of this unbinding?”
sn38.1:2.6: “Yes, there is a path, there is a practice for the realization of this unbinding.”
sn38.1:2.7: “Then what is the path, what is the practice for the realization of this unbinding?”
sn38.1:2.8: “Precisely this noble eightfold path, my friend:
sn38.1:2.9: right view, right resolve, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, right concentration.
sn38.1:2.10: This is the path, this is the practice for the realization of this unbinding.”
sn38.1:2.11: “It’s an auspicious path, my friend, an auspicious practice for the realization of this unbinding—
sn38.1:2.12: enough for the sake of heedfulness.”
